label: Labels, targets, rewards & preferences

node.label: Labels, targets, rewards & preferences
node.definition: External or constructed signals specify desired predictions, actions, rankings, or behavior.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Labels, targets, rewards & preferences is to connect Statistics & experimental design, Game theory & decision theory to downstream capabilities including Supervised learning, Semi-supervised & weak supervision, Reinforcement learning.
limitations: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Statistics & experimental design, Game theory & decision theory.

sentence_units.text: External or constructed signals specify desired predictions, actions, rankings, or behavior.

The role of Labels, targets, rewards & preferences is to connect Statistics & experimental design, Game theory & decision theory to downstream capabilities including Supervised learning, Semi-supervised & weak supervision, Reinforcement learning.

Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Statistics & experimental design, Game theory & decision theory.
